items.briefing:
With the Socceroos' World Cup campaign underway, cybercriminals are exploiting fan excitement through fake streaming sites, phishing links, and fraudulent ticket offers — Australians searching for match coverage should stick to official, trusted sources. Separately, the Asia-Pacific region is being hit hardest by identity fraud globally, meaning Australian staff should stay alert to suspicious requests for personal or account information, whether by email, text, or phone.

A scam technique called ClickFix is also gaining momentum, tricking users into running malicious commands on both Windows and Mac computers by disguising them as routine fixes. **Practical tip:** If a website or pop-up asks you to copy and paste a command into your computer, stop immediately and contact your IT team.

featured.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
featured.quote: New SMS Sender ID Register directly protects Australian consumers from increasing scam threats.
